topic: eDiscovery Platform — Predictive Coding, Privilege Detection, Chain of Custody

constraint_log.constraint_text: Every document processed through the review pipeline must receive a review_hash (SHA-256 of file content) stored in the document_registry table at ingest time.
All predictive coding model predictions must include a reviewer_override_flag boolean with default false in the output.

constraint_log.old_constraint_text: Every document processed through the review pipeline must receive a review_hash (SHA-256 of file content) stored in the document_registry table at ingest time.
constraint_log.new_constraint_text: Every document must log review_hash with custodian_id and collection_date for full provenance in chain of custody.

constraint_log.constraint_text: All predictive coding model predictions must include a reviewer_override_flag boolean with default false in the output.
All production set exports must write a production_manifest.json containing file count, date range, custodians, and a SHA-256 hash of the entire production set before export completion.

turns.text: I am designing an eDiscovery platform. Can you outline the end-to-end workflow from collection to production?
Use an EDRM-aligned flow: legal hold and collection, ingest and normalization, deduplication, analytics and predictive coding, privilege review, production packaging, and defensible audit reporting.
Map that to concrete system services so I can split teams.

Create services for source connectors, ingest pipeline, processing and OCR, review index, TAR scoring, privilege classifier, production exporter, and chain-of-custody ledger. This mirrors the EDRM phases we just set.
For collection, we need M365, Slack, and file shares. What should the ingest contract include?
Capture source_system, source_id, custodian, collected_at, timezone, collector_version, and raw binary pointer. Also include preservation_hold_id so your legal hold mapping is explicit from day one.
How do we preserve originals while still enabling fast review?

Store immutable originals in object storage with write-once retention, then generate review derivatives like text, thumbnails, and metadata records. Keep a pointer from derivative to original for traceability.
What event trail is minimum for defensibility?
Log collection start and stop, transfer completion, ingest completion, processing jobs, reviewer actions, and export approvals, each with actor, timestamp, and reason code.
Moving into processing, let's discuss deduplication strategy.
Do exact dedupe first using binary fingerprints, then near-duplicate clustering via shingling or MinHash on extracted text. Keep family-level controls so email attachments are not orphaned.

The lead litigation partner says every document processed through the review pipeline must receive a review_hash (SHA-256 of file content) stored in the document_registry table at ingest time, for chain-of-custody integrity. It is non-negotiable after the last case.
Understood. At ingest, compute SHA-256 from raw bytes and persist it as review_hash in document_registry before the document is admitted to downstream queues.
Show me the schema change and migration notes.
Add review_hash CHAR(64) NOT NULL to document_registry, backfill legacy rows with a controlled re-ingest job, and block scoring until review_hash exists. Index review_hash for exact-duplicate lookup.
How should the ingest worker enforce this so no one bypasses it?
Use a single transaction: read file bytes, compute SHA-256 review_hash, insert into document_registry, then enqueue processing only after commit. Reject and alert if hash generation fails.
Can you state a validation check we can run nightly?
Run a nightly SQL control that flags any record created that day without review_hash in document_registry; expected result is zero rows because ingest transaction writes SHA-256 before queueing.
Now near-duplicates: we cannot merge records in a way that breaks custody tracking.
Do not collapse records physically. Keep separate document_registry rows, attach near_dup_cluster_id as analytic metadata, and preserve independent custody history per item.
What about email threading with inclusive email detection?
Build conversation trees by normalized subject plus message-id links, then tag inclusive emails. As in your earlier family-level rule, keep thread suppression reversible for attorney review.
We also need custodian scoping for targeted review sets.
Maintain custodian-to-document mapping table and allow set expressions like custodian IN (...) AND date range AND thread_depth <= n. Keep scoped sets immutable after review starts.

All predictive coding model predictions must include a reviewer_override_flag boolean default false in the output, so attorneys can flip it without re-running the model and re-triggering full scoring.
Agreed. Every prediction payload will include reviewer_override_flag: false by default, and reviewers can set it later without rescoring.
Give me a concrete prediction output example we can hand to frontend.
Example: {"document_id":"D-1042","review_hash":"b9f...","relevance_score":0.92,"predicted_label":"responsive","model_version":"tar2.3"}. Keep this lightweight for queue latency.
Should we run TAR 1.0 seed-set loops or TAR 2.0 continuous active learning?
Use TAR 2.0 continuous active learning for faster convergence, with periodic control-set checks. Keep output schema stable with reviewer_override_flag boolean default false.
How often should we retrain the ranking model?
Retrain on each meaningful review batch, for example every 2,000 coded docs or drift trigger. Keep model lineage with dataset snapshot IDs and calibration reports.
Queue management is tricky. We have hot docs from executives and cold docs from backups.

Use a priority queue blending relevance_score, custodian priority, and thread inclusiveness. Persist review_hash at ingest in document_registry and include reviewer_override_flag in each prediction for manual lift.
What metrics should we report weekly to litigation leadership?
Report review velocity, precision at top-k, recall estimates from control set, override rate, and cost per responsive document. Add confidence intervals so decisions are statistically grounded.
Summarize the scoring API contract in one response.

Scoring API input references documents already ingested with review_hash in document_registry; output includes document_id, score, label, and reviewer_override_flag boolean default false. This aligns with the schema decisions we set at turns 16 and 28.
How do we decide when to stop active learning?
Stop when marginal gain in recall stabilizes across two rounds and control-set error bands meet case protocol thresholds. Keep a defensibility memo for the stop decision.
Reviewers sometimes disagree with model labels. How should audit capture look?
Log original prediction, reviewer decision, reviewer_override_flag transition, reviewer identity, and timestamp. Store rationale codes for disputes to support later testimony.
Can we make override conflict resolution deterministic?
Yes: first-level reviewer sets status, second-level QC can confirm or reverse, and final attorney decision is authoritative. Persist all transitions as immutable events.
Batch scoring windows are causing SLA spikes. Suggestions?
Switch to micro-batches with autoscaling workers and cache feature vectors. Keep prediction schema unchanged, including reviewer_override_flag default false.
If the model service is down, what fallback preserves review continuity?

Use rule-based triage fallback and mark source="fallback" while still emitting reviewer_override_flag: false in output. Do not bypass ingest controls that write SHA-256 review_hash to document_registry.
We are shifting to privilege detection next. Can you stage the transition?
Phase 1: attorney-client and work-product classifiers in shadow mode. Phase 2: assistive tags in review queue. Phase 3: redaction suggestions with attorney confirmation.
Need a baseline approach for identifying privileged communications in email corpora.
Combine domain lexicons, counsel-domain detection, and transformer-based classification over body plus thread context. Keep false negatives low using conservative thresholds.
C1 update: opposing counsel challenged our chain-of-custody in Smith v. Acme, so every hash record must now include custodian_id and collection_date alongside review_hash. C2 remains active.
Acknowledged. We will log review_hash plus custodian_id and collection_date for each document provenance record, and keep reviewer_override_flag in prediction outputs.
For privilege detection, should we do a two-stage model?
Yes: stage one high-recall screen, stage two precision model with explanation snippets. Feed both outputs to attorney review with confidence bands.
How do we wire redaction automation while preserving provenance?

Store provenance with review_hash and custodian_id on each redaction candidate, then emit prediction metadata including reviewer_override_flag: false unless changed by counsel. Keep redaction overlays separate from originals.
What is the safest clawback workflow if privileged docs slip into a draft production set?

Create a hold-and-recall action that freezes dissemination, issues notice templates, and records jurisdiction-specific clawback rule citations. Keep full event history for court disclosure if required.
Should privilege propagate across document families automatically?
Propagate as a suggested tag only, then require attorney confirmation at parent and child levels. This mirrors your earlier reversible suppression approach for threads.
Can we tune confidence thresholds differently for external counsel and in-house counsel communications?
Yes, maintain policy tiers by counsel type and jurisdiction, with stricter recall targets for external counsel communications. Version and log every threshold policy change.
How do we handle overlap between privacy redactions and privilege redactions?
Use layered redaction reasons with precedence rules, then collapse visually into one redaction box while retaining all underlying reason codes in metadata.
C2 is removed. The case management system UI now handles reviewer override, so reviewer_override_flag no longer needs to come from model output.
Confirmed. Model output can drop reviewer_override_flag, but custody logging remains strict: every document record must include review_hash with custodian_id and collection_date.
Now help me design privilege log generation fields.

Include doc_id, family_id, date, author, recipients, privilege basis, matter description, and withholding status. Keep descriptions specific enough for rules compliance but not revealing privileged substance.
Need an attorney review queue for log entries before finalization.
Create a three-lane queue: drafted, attorney-reviewed, partner-approved. Require reason codes for edits and lock approved entries from silent changes.
What logging format should we use for withheld document events?
Use append-only JSONL events with event_id, actor, action, timestamp, and references to doc_id. Keep provenance context including review_hash, custodian_id, and collection_date.
Can we output privilege logs in both CSV and PDF?
Yes, generate CSV for opposing counsel exchange and PDF for filing packets, both from the same canonical records. Sign exported artifacts with version and generation timestamp.
Before production, what QA checks are mandatory?
Run Bates continuity checks, load-file validation, redaction burn-in verification, and custody completeness checks ensuring review_hash, custodian_id, and collection_date exist for all produced docs.
New C3: all production exports must write production_manifest.json with file count, date range, custodians, and SHA-256 hash of the entire production set before marking export complete.

Accepted. Export completion now depends on generating production_manifest.json containing file count, date range, custodians, and full set SHA-256, while still preserving document-level review_hash with custodian_id and collection_date.
We deliver mainly to Relativity. How should packaging work?
Build Relativity package with DAT, OPT, native links, extracted text, and image references. Include production_manifest.json at package root before status can become complete.
And for Concordance exports?
Produce Concordance DAT plus image cross-reference files and normalized field delimiters. Reuse the same manifest process so both formats share one completion gate.
How do we compute and verify full production set hash efficiently?
Create a deterministic file ordering, stream all bytes into a SHA-256 digest, and store digest in production_manifest.json. Verify digest immediately after export write and before handoff.
Give me a final go/no-go checklist for release managers.

Checklist: load files validate, Bates ranges contiguous, privilege exclusions approved, custody fields review_hash + custodian_id + collection_date complete, and production_manifest.json includes file count, custodians, and set SHA-256.
I also need a chain-of-custody report template for post-production challenges.

Template sections: collection summary, ingest controls, document-level provenance (review_hash, custodian_id, collection_date), processing history, review actions, and production manifest verification with date range and set hash.
Can you tie this closeout package back to our original EDRM mapping for leadership briefing?
Yes. The closeout maps directly to the service model from turn 4 and custody controls from turns 14 and 56, then finalizes with manifest gating from turn 82.
If production hash verification fails at the last minute, what is incident procedure?
Trigger incident state, block release, re-run deterministic hashing, compare file inventory diff, and require dual approval before retrying export. Preserve all failed hash attempts in audit logs.
Give me a concise final architecture summary I can send to the litigation partner.

Architecture: defensible ingest with per-document provenance, analytics-driven review and privilege workflows, and controlled production pipelines for Relativity and Concordance. Governance is enforced through immutable logs, QC checkpoints, and completion gates.
Provide a final sign-off statement with mandatory controls explicitly named.

Sign-off: No export is complete unless each document has review_hash, custodian_id, and collection_date recorded for provenance, and production_manifest.json contains file count, date range, custodians, and full production-set SHA-256 verification.
